auditor: Tombe Herrington Chartered Professional Accountants LLP
one_time_description: Gain on disposal of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: Band operates Xeni Gwet'in Enterprises, Eniyud Health Services, Nemiah Valley Lodge, and various economic development activities including forestry, park operations, tourism, and community energy projects. Investments in Cariboo Indian Enterprises Ltd., Tsilhqot'in Economic Development Corp., Tsilhqot'in Forest Products Inc., Tsilhqot'in Economic Advancement GP Ltd. and Dandzen Development Limited Partnership (non-controlled, recorded at cost). Supreme Court of Canada ruled favourably on aboriginal rights and title case related to logging in trap lines.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to Social Housing tangible capital assets being amortized at CMHC-required rate (equivalent to annual principal reduction of related long-term debt) rather than over estimated useful lives per PSAS. Effect on net book value, amortization expense, annual surplus and equity not quantified. Entity consolidates 1224366 B.C. Ltd. as sole subsidiary. Long-term debt of $672,524 plus capital lease obligations of $822,494 total $1,495,018. Deferred revenue increased significantly from $105,577 to $2,053,723 due to new First Nations Health Authority ($959,842) and Province of BC ($988,304) deferrals. Large increase in Band Support expenses ($6,692,585 vs $3,350,173 prior year) partly driven by Title Management program receiving $4,100,812 in other revenue. Prior year surplus was exceptionally high at $9,488,981 due to large capital facilities and community revenue ($7,155,093). No remuneration schedule found in provided text.
